# DedTxt

> A dead simple, dependency-free plain-text editor. Runs in any modern browser
> and installs as a PWA (https://dedtxt.app/). No accounts, no tracking, works
> offline. Raw bytes in, raw bytes out — UTF-8 by default, no BOM, no
> line-ending munging.

DedTxt is intentionally minimal: a single textarea and your file, with find &
replace, always-on word wrap, a line-number gutter, and silent save on Chromium
browsers (via the File System Access API). It is built from vanilla HTML, CSS,
and ES modules with no runtime dependencies. A Tauri desktop build exists in the
source, but native installers are paused for now in favor of the PWA.

## Project

- [Web app](https://dedtxt.app/): the editor itself.
- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/cportka/ded-txt): code, issues, and pull requests.
- [README](https://github.com/cportka/ded-txt/blob/main/README.md): overview, build steps, keyboard shortcuts.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/cportka/ded-txt/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): release history.
- [Privacy](https://github.com/cportka/ded-txt/blob/main/PRIVACY.md): the app collects no data.

## Contributing

- [Contributing guide](https://github.com/cportka/ded-txt/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [Future ideas](https://github.com/cportka/ded-txt/blob/main/FUTURE.md)

## Notes for agents

- DedTxt stores no user data on any server; files stay local to the device.
- The web app updates via the service-worker lifecycle, not by polling a version
  file — a cache-first fetch would serve a stale copy.
